_README: contest-eval alert catalog + per-type toggles. One object per alert TYPE. 'enabled' gates whether the poller builds alerts of that type at all (disabled => silently skipped, never POSTed). 'severity' (critical|warning|info) OVERRIDES the dynamically-computed default severity in alerts.build_alerts when set to a non-null value; set severity to null (or delete the key) to keep the dynamic mapping. NOTE: severity also drives verdict-seam routing (alerts.is_ambiguous routes warning-level web_paste/recurring_pair/peer_copy_cluster to the human seam), so forcing a severity here can change whether a type is routed for human review. Missing file => every type enabled with dynamic severity (current/legacy behavior). FIRST-ATTEMPT FLAGS: 'first_attempt_solve' fires when a candidate got a problem ACCEPTED on their FIRST submission attempt (zero prior wrong attempts); 'tough_first_attempt' is emitted INSTEAD when that first-attempt solve is on a TOUGH problem. TOUGH = the challenge slug/id is in the operator-marked 'tough_questions' list below OR it is data-derived hard (<=10 solvers); the manual list WINS/AUGMENTS the data derivation. 'fast_solve' is the deprecated former name of first_attempt_solve and is still accepted here as an ALIAS (it seeds first_attempt_solve's config if first_attempt_solve is absent), but new alerts are only emitted under first_attempt_solve / tough_first_attempt.

peer_copy_cluster.notes: 2+ distinct users with identical (skeleton) code on the same MED/HARD problem — direct peer copying.

recurring_pair.notes: A pair sharing identical code across 2+ problems OR 1+ hard problem — conclusive collusion between two candidates.

web_paste.notes: Accepted code carries web/editorial provenance signatures (smart quotes, NBSP, class-Solution, etc.) — likely pasted from an online source.

first_attempt_solve.notes: Candidate got a problem ACCEPTED on their FIRST submission attempt (zero prior wrong attempts on that problem) — on a NORMAL (non-tough) problem. Metadata corroborator, not a standalone flag.

tough_first_attempt.notes: A first-attempt accepted solve on a TOUGH problem (operator-marked in tough_questions OR data-derived hard). The real 'solved a tough question on the first attempt' FLAG — emitted instead of first_attempt_solve for tough problems.
